text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Eggs are graded by the hen raising method, such as free range and battery caged.","claimType":"definitional","quote":", eggs are graded by the hen raising method, [free sentence directly states that size grading of chicken eggs is done to facilitate sales.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Some maxi eggs may have double-yolks and some farms separate out double-yolk eggs for special sale.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Some maxi eggs may have double-yolks and some farms separate out double-yolk eggs for special sale.[\\[116\\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggs_as_food#cite_note-116)","entailment":"The source reports the occurrence of double-yolk maxi eggs and that farms may segregate them for distinct marketing.","population":"maxi eggs","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"presence of double-yolks and segregation for special sale","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"may have","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Eggshell color is a largely cosmetic issue with no effect on egg quality or taste.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Although eggshell color is a largely cosmetic issue, with no effect on egg quality or taste, it is a major issue in production due to regional and national preferences for specific colors, and the results of such preferences on demand.","entailment":"The passage explicitly claims that color does not influence quality or taste, describing it as cosmetic.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"no effect on egg quality or taste","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"no effect","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"In most regions of the United States, chicken eggs are generally white.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"in most regions of the United States, chicken eggs are generally white.","entailment":"The source directly observes that white eggs predominate in most U.S. regions.","population":"United States","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"egg color (white) predominance","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"generally","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Brown eggs are preferred in China, Costa Rica, Ireland, France, and the United listed countries.","population":"China, Costa Rica, Ireland, France, United Kingdom","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"preference for brown eggs","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Research conducted by a French institute in the 1970s demonstrated blue chicken eggs from the Chilean araucana fowl may be stronger and more resilient to breakage.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Research conducted by a French institute in the 1970s demonstrated blue chicken eggs from the Chilean [araucana](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Araucana \"Araucana\") fowl may be stronger and more resilient to breakage.[\\[120\\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggs_as_food#cite_note-newscientist-120)","entailment":"The source reports a study suggesting that blue eggs from araucana chickens have greater strength and breakage resistance.","population":"blue chicken eggs from Chilean araucana fowl","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"strength and resilience to breakage","timeframe":"1970s","quantifiers":"may be stronger and more resilient","modality":"associational"}]}
